assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies dev clusters on Rapid channel and prod clusters on Stable channel throughout, with specific sections addressing dev environment validation first

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Response mentions mandatory resource requests multiple times, notes Google manages infrastructure layer, and includes checks for resource request requirements

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl commands for checking deprecated API usage and mentions GKE deprecation insights dashboard

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All containers have resource requests (mandatory in Autopilot)' and includes specific commands to check for this

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Response includes checks for PDBs, bare pods with kubectl command, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds for graceful shutdown

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Response includes specific gcloud command to verify control plane version and mentions verifying nodes upgraded automatically

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Response has extensive workload health checks including deployments, pods, StatefulSets, DaemonSets, and application validation

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions dev clusters should upgrade first via Rapid channel and recommends using them as canary environment

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Response mentions 72h advance warning but does not discuss the GKE release schedule providing ~1 month advance notice for planning
Response includes extensive observability sections with monitoring dashboards, metrics collection, error rates, and API latency checks

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance exclusions only at the cluster level for timing control, not node-level exclusions, which is appropriate for Autopilot
